steps.title: Identify what supports the hypothesis
steps.content: Martinez and colleagues hypothesized that P. rufifrons' greater vulnerability (from gleaning) makes it more sensitive to predator warnings from neighboring species. The key signal: P. rufifrons should show stronger response to local bird alarms than T. surinamus.

steps.title: Match the predicted contrast
steps.content: Choice D shows this directly: when local bird alarm calls are played, P. rufifrons displays predator-avoidance behavior whereas T. surinamus does not, confirming that the more vulnerable foraging strategy correlates with increased sensitivity to neighbor warnings. Choices A and B show both species responding equally to at least one stimulus, while C shows the opposite pattern. This matches choice D — D is correct.
